parts.text: Let's make a new release based on the changes since v1.0.0
I'll load the release skill and gather information about changes since v1.0.0 to plan this release.

state.output:
<skill_content name="release">
# Skill: release

Your job is to guide the user through creating a release for this project. Releases use annotated git tags with a `v` prefix (e.g. `v1.1.0`) and are built by the GoReleaser GitHub Actions workflow. Release notes are set explicitly via `gh release edit` — do NOT rely on the workflow to populate them.

## Rules

- Always create an **annotated** tag (`git tag -a`), never a lightweight tag.
- Tag format is `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` following [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
- The changelog MUST follow [Keep a Changelog 1.1.0](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/) format.
- Valid changelog categories: Added, Changed, Deprecated, Removed, Fixed, Security.
- DO NOT push the tag until the user confirms.
- DO NOT skip asking the user for the version number, tag annotation message, and changelog review.

## Steps

1. **Gather context**
   - Check the current branch and ensure it is clean (`git status`).
   - List existing tags (`git tag --sort=-v:refname`) to determine the next version.
   - Collect the commit log since the last tag (or all commits if this is the first release).

2. **Ask the user**
   - What version number to use.
   - What the annotated tag message should say.
   - Whether the auto-generated changelog looks correct or needs edits.

3. **Categorize commits** into Keep a Changelog sections:
   - `Added` — new features and capabilities.
   - `Changed` — changes to existing functionality.
   - `Deprecated` — features marked for removal.
   - `Removed` — features that were removed.
   - `Fixed` — bug fixes.
   - `Security` — vulnerability fixes.
   - Exclude commits prefixed with `docs:`, `test:`, `ci:`, or merge commits.

4. **Update CHANGELOG.md**
   - Prepend the new release section under the `# Changelog` header.
   - Add a reference link at the bottom of the file using the Keep a Changelog format:
     - First release: `[1.0.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/releases/tag/v1.0.0`
     - Subsequent releases: `[1.1.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.0.0...v1.1.0`
   - Commit the changelog update before tagging.

5. **Create the annotated tag** on the changelog commit.

6. **Push** the commit and tag to origin only after the user confirms. The `v*` tag push triggers the GoReleaser workflow which builds binaries and creates the GitHub release.

7. **Set release notes via `gh`**
   - After the tag is pushed, extract the version's section from `CHANGELOG.md` (excluding the heading and reference link footer) and write it to a temporary file.
   - Run `gh release edit vX.Y.Z --notes-file <tmp-file>` to set the release description.
   - This is the authoritative source for release notes — do NOT rely on the workflow's awk extraction.

## Reference

- GoReleaser config: `.goreleaser.yml` (auto-changelog is disabled)
- Release workflow: `.github/workflows/release.yml` (builds binaries; do NOT rely on it for release notes)
- Changelog: `CHANGELOG.md`

Base directory for this skill: file:///Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/.opencode/skills/release
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>

<content>1: # Changelog
2: 
3: All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
4: 
5: The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
6: and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
7: 
8: ## [1.0.0] - 2026-03-02
9: 
10: ### Added
11: - Core CLI with `run`, `agents list`, `agents show`, `agents init`, `agents edit`, `gc`, and `config init` commands
12: - TOML-based agent configuration with SKILL.md context support
13: - Multi-provider LLM support: Anthropic, OpenAI, and Ollama
14: - Tool-calling system with `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `list_directory`, and `run_command` tools
15: - Sub-agent orchestration with `call_agent` tool and depth-limited execution
16: - Agent memory system with XDG-compliant data storage and garbage collection
17: - Path security: `~` and `$VAR` expansion for user-supplied paths
18: - Stdin piping support for composing with Unix tools
19: - Dry-run mode and per-tool verbose logging
20: - Docker containerization support
21: - CI workflows: lint, test, build, and GoReleaser
22: 
23: ### Fixed
24: - Arg validation UX and skill path resolution
25: - staticcheck/errcheck lint issues
26: - Duplicate error silencing, glob validation, and nil ExitError guard
27: 
28: [1.0.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/releases/tag/v1.0.0

(End of file - total 28 lines)

metadata.preview:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [1.0.0] - 2026-03-02

### Added
- Core CLI with `run`, `agents list`, `agents show`, `agents init`, `agents edit`, `gc`, and `config init` commands
- TOML-based agent configuration with SKILL.md context support
- Multi-provider LLM support: Anthropic, OpenAI, and Ollama
- Tool-calling system with `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `list_directory`, and `run_command` tools
- Sub-agent orchestration with `call_agent` tool and depth-limited execution
- Agent memory system with XDG-compliant data storage and garbage collection
- Path security: `~` and `$VAR` expansion for user-supplied paths
- Stdin piping support for composing with Unix tools
- Dry-run mode and per-tool verbose logging
- Docker containerization support

parts.text:
Good. The working tree is clean on `master`. Let me categorize the 8 commits since v1.0.0.

Here's my proposed plan for the release:

---

## Release Plan

**Branch:** `master` (clean)
**Previous version:** `v1.0.0`
**Commits since v1.0.0:** 8

### Categorized Changelog

**Added:**
- MCP tool support (GAI-0009, #15)
- `web_search` built-in tool using Tavily Search API (#14)
- `url_fetch` built-in tool (GAI-0007, #13)
- JSON `tool_call_details` in run output (#10)
- Refusal detection in JSON output (Issue #8)
- Executable examples (#3)

**Changed:**
- Docker docs for running a single agent with selective mounts (#4)

**Excluded** (matches `ci:` / internal tooling pattern):
- `f631672` — Update release skill (internal skill config, not user-facing)

---

Before I proceed, I have a few questions:

questions.question: What version number should this release use?

options.label: v1.1.0
options.description: Minor release — new features added, no breaking changes

options.label: v2.0.0
options.description: Major release — if you consider MCP support or other changes breaking

questions.question: Does the changelog categorization above look correct? Specifically: (1) Docker docs (#4) under 'Changed' vs 'Added', and (2) excluding the release skill commit as non-user-facing.

options.label: Looks good
options.description: The categorization is correct as proposed

questions.question: What should the annotated tag message say?

options.label: Release v1.1.0
options.description: Simple standard tag message (version will match your choice above)

options.label: Release v1.1.0: MCP, tools, and output enhancements

You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
state.title: Asked 3 questions

parts.text:
Great. Here's the finalized plan:

---

## Release v1.1.0 — Execution Plan

### 1. Update `CHANGELOG.md`
Prepend a new `## [1.1.0] - 2026-03-06` section with:

**Added:**
- MCP tool support
- `web_search` built-in tool using Tavily Search API
- `url_fetch` built-in tool
- JSON `tool_call_details` in run output
- Refusal detection in JSON output
- Executable examples

**Changed:**
- Docker docs for running a single agent with selective mounts

Add a comparison link at the bottom:
`[1.1.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.0.0...v1.1.0`

### 2. Commit the changelog
`git add CHANGELOG.md && git commit -m "Update changelog for v1.1.0"`

### 3. Create annotated tag
`git tag -a v1.1.0 -m "Release v1.1.0"`

### 4. Push (after your confirmation)
`git push origin master --follow-tags`

### 5. Set release notes via `gh`
Extract the v1.1.0 section from CHANGELOG.md and run:
`gh release edit v1.1.0 --notes-file <tmp-file>`

---

Shall I proceed with execution?

# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [1.0.0] - 2026-03-02

### Added
- Core CLI with `run`, `agents list`, `agents show`, `agents init`, `agents edit`, `gc`, and `config init` commands
- TOML-based agent configuration with SKILL.md context support
- Multi-provider LLM support: Anthropic, OpenAI, and Ollama
- Tool-calling system with `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `list_directory`, and `run_command` tools
- Sub-agent orchestration with `call_agent` tool and depth-limited execution
- Agent memory system with XDG-compliant data storage and garbage collection
- Path security: `~` and `$VAR` expansion for user-supplied paths
- Stdin piping support for composing with Unix tools
- Dry-run mode and per-tool verbose logging
- Docker containerization support
- CI workflows: lint, test, build, and GoReleaser

### Fixed
- Arg validation UX and skill path resolution
- staticcheck/errcheck lint issues
- Duplicate error silencing, glob validation, and nil ExitError guard

[1.0.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/releases/tag/v1.0.0

24: - Core CLI with `run`, `agents list`, `agents show`, `agents init`, `agents edit`, `gc`, and `config init` commands
25: - TOML-based agent configuration with SKILL.md context support
26: - Multi-provider LLM support: Anthropic, OpenAI, and Ollama
27: - Tool-calling system with `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `list_directory`, and `run_command` tools
28: - Sub-agent orchestration with `call_agent` tool and depth-limited execution
29: - Agent memory system with XDG-compliant data storage and garbage collection
30: - Path security: `~` and `$VAR` expansion for user-supplied paths
31: - Stdin piping support for composing with Unix tools
32: - Dry-run mode and per-tool verbose logging
33: - Docker containerization support
34: - CI workflows: lint, test, build, and GoReleaser
35: 
36: ### Fixed
37: - Arg validation UX and skill path resolution
38: - staticcheck/errcheck lint issues
39: - Duplicate error silencing, glob validation, and nil ExitError guard
40: 
41: [1.1.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/compare/v1.0.0...v1.1.0
42: [1.0.0]: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/releases/tag/v1.0.0

(End of file - total 42 lines)
</content>
state.title: CHANGELOG.md

metadata.preview:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [1.1.0] - 2026-03-06

### Added
- MCP tool support
- `web_search` built-in tool using Tavily Search API
- `url_fetch` built-in tool
- JSON `tool_call_details` in run output
- Refusal detection in JSON output
- Executable examples

### Changed
- Docker docs for running a single agent with selective mounts
